sentences.english: When did you first realize that something was wrong at Veridian Pharmaceuticals?
sentences.korean: 베리디언 제약에서 뭔가 잘못됐다는 걸 처음 알게 된 건 언제였나요?

sentences.english: It wasn't one dramatic moment.
It was a slow accumulation of things that didn't add up.
sentences.korean: 말이 안 되는 것들이 천천히 쌓인 거였습니다.

sentences.english: I was working as a data analyst on the clinical trial team for their flagship drug, Corexa.
My job was to compile the trial results and flag any anomalies.
sentences.korean: 제 일은 임상시험 결과를 정리하고 이상을 표시하는 거였어요.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?

sentences.english: Around month eight of the trial, I started noticing patterns in the adverse event data that should have triggered a safety review.
What kind of patterns?
A statistically significant increase in cardiac events among participants over sixty-five — elevated heart rates, arrhythmias, and in three cases, what appeared to be drug-induced cardiac arrest.
sentences.korean: 65세 이상 참가자들 사이에서 통계적으로 유의미한 심장 사건 증가가 있었습니다.

sentences.english: In a trial of that size, with that demographic, those numbers were alarming.
sentences.korean: 그 규모의 임상시험에서, 그 인구통계로는, 그 숫자가 놀라웠습니다.

sentences.english: And what happened when you raised the concern?

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: How to Make Friends in a New City

sentences.english: I submitted a formal internal report to my supervisor and to the head of clinical operations.
sentences.korean: 상사와 임상운영 책임자에게 공식 내부 보고서를 제출했어요.

sentences.english: The response was... instructive.
My supervisor thanked me for my thoroughness and then told me that the senior team had already reviewed the data and determined that the events fell within an acceptable risk margin.
sentences.korean: 상사가 꼼꼼함에 감사하다고 하더니, 고위팀이 이미 데이터를 검토했고 수용 가능한 위험 범위 내라고 판단했다고 했어요.

sentences.english: No further action was needed.
Did you believe that assessment?

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?

sentences.english: No. I went back and looked at the raw data again, and I found that several adverse events had been reclassified.
sentences.korean: 아니요. 다시 원시 데이터를 살펴봤더니 여러 이상반응이 재분류되어 있었습니다.

sentences.english: Cardiac events that I had flagged were reclassified as "pre-existing conditions" or "unrelated to treatment." The effect was to lower the apparent adverse event rate below the threshold that would require the company to notify the FDA.
sentences.korean: 제가 표시했던 심장 사건들이 '기존 질환' 또는 '치료와 무관'으로 재분류되어 있었습니다.

sentences.english: So the data was being manipulated.
I wouldn't have used that word at the time.
sentences.korean: 그때는 그 단어를 쓰지 않았을 거예요.

sentences.english: I told myself there might be a legitimate clinical justification I wasn't aware of.
sentences.korean: 제가 모르는 정당한 임상적 근거가 있을 수도 있다고 스스로에게 말했거든요.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?

sentences.english: But when I requested access to the reclassification memos, I was denied.
And a week later, I was transferred to a different project entirely.
sentences.korean: 그리고 1주일 후, 완전히 다른 프로젝트로 전환 배치됐습니다.

sentences.english: That must have been a turning point.
It was. I realized that if I stayed inside the system, nothing would change.
sentences.korean: 그랬어요. 시스템 안에 머물면 아무것도 바뀌지 않는다는 걸 깨달았습니다.

sentences.english: The incentive structure was designed to suppress exactly the kind of information I was trying to surface.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: How to Deal with Jet Lag

sentences.english: So I contacted the Office of the Inspector General and submitted everything I had.
sentences.korean: 그래서 감찰관실에 연락해서 가지고 있던 모든 것을 제출했습니다.

sentences.english: And what was the personal cost?
I lost my career at Veridian.
I was labeled a disgruntled employee.
sentences.korean: 불만 직원으로 낙인찍히고, 일부 동료들이 저와 말을 끊었습니다.

sentences.english: Some colleagues stopped speaking to me.
But three months after my report, the FDA opened a formal investigation.
And last year, Corexa was pulled from the market.
So I would make the same choice again.
sentences.korean: 그래서 같은 선택을 다시 할 거예요.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
